message:
fix(wasm-demo): defer Monaco editor initialization to prevent DOM access before ready

Fixes critical browser crash: "Cannot read properties of null (reading 'addEventListener')"

Root cause:
- app.js line 24 called mustGet('editor') at module top-level
- mustGet is defined at line 314, AFTER this call
- Module executes top-to-bottom, causing undefined function error
- Even if mustGet existed, DOM elements not ready during module parse

Solution:
- Defer editorContainer initialization using DOMContentLoaded check
- Use document.getElementById directly instead of mustGet
- Wrap Monaco initialization in initMonaco() function
- Handle both loading and interactive document states
- Add null check with warning if editor element missing

This ensures:
✓ No function calls before definition
✓ DOM elements exist before access
✓ Graceful fallback if editor element missing
✓ No race conditions with DOM parsing

Related: Previous commit fixed SSR issues in React Three Fiber components,
but this crash was in the WASM iframe demo, not the main Next.js app.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code (https://claude.com/claude-code)
